programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Mike works with the Army Corps of Engineers on a massive project to restore essential flow of fresh water to the Florida Everglades. He also joins scientists, python hunters and the Miccosuke Tribe to eliminate invasive species.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: At Cirque Du Soileil in Las Vegas, Mike embeds with the talented teams that maintain the amazing machines behind Cirque Du Soleil's stage shows.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Diving into retrofits on the world's only underwater habitat, where NASA astronauts train for space missions; Mike joins a crack repair team who breathe new life into this oceanic environment after a global pandemic shut it down.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.sub-title: US Coast Guard: Safeguarding The Seas
programme.desc: Mike helps fix the vessels that the U.S. Coast Guard use to keep the oceans safe; he replaces key parts of ship engines, removes a large crane from a buoy tender, and fixes a helicopter for a critical exercise.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Defy gravity in an Amsterdam high-rise that hangs 164 feet above the water; marvel at three interconnected towers in Chicago that have a road running through them; examine the revolutionary framework of Paris' controversial Pompidou Centre.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Architects and engineers build an eco-friendly convention center on Vancouver's waterfront, a lift bridge in Bordeaux and a cocoon-shaped skyscraper in Tokyo each with unique challenges like marine habitats and massive spans.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A dazzling new 1400 foot tapering skyscraper is built right next to New York's Grand Central. A curving wine museum requires ingenious engineering in Bordeaux, France. And possibly the world's most glamorous parking garage.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The engineering secrets behind a massive sky garden in Singapore, the world's most slender tower in Brighton, and a railway station in the shadow of Vesuvius.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A new Olympic and Paralympic museum tests engineers with its trailblazing accessible design. A dazzling curved glass skyscraper battles gravity in Milan. And a student center built under a noisy railroad.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine what caused the Zeebrugge ferry tragedy and the Costa Concordia cruise ship disaster; what were the consequences, and how could they have been avoided?

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On 20 August 2011, First Air Flight 6560, a charter flight, crashes near its destination of Resolute Bay Airport, Resolute, Nunavut, Canada, in poor weather conditions; out of the fifteen passengers and crew members, only three survived.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at accidents and incidents where problems with the interface between crew members and onboard avionics are a factor, and the causes of those problems.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Midair tragedies are particularly horrifying when they occur over a community, and rain terror on the people below.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a cargo plane crashes at a U.S. airfield in Afghanistan, investigators reach a conclusion that ultimately proves incorrect.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a commuter turboprop plane crashes at an Irish airport, investigators uncover a startling gap in aviation safety.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Alaska Airlines 261 nosedives into the Pacific leaving investigators stumped; a rare storm and a deadly design flaw intersect cause American Eagle 4184 to fall from the sky; investigators suspect a fatal flaw in the Boeing 737.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When basketball legend Kobe Bryant is killed in a helicopter crash in Calabasas, investigators examine the data, the wreckage, and the pilot's actions for clues.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A design flaw sends American Eagle Flight 41-84 smashing into a cornfield outside of Chicago, killing all 68 people on board.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Recounting some of maritime warfare's explosions, from world wars and Vietnam to the nuclear tests at Bikini Atoll.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most effective war machines: the Viking longship, seeing how its design evolved from swift war canoe to versatile and feared ship that would change the course of history.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at the most famous wooden warships over the course of 500 years.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The ships that made mighty empires, from the lone wolves that patrolled the high seas for imperial Britain to the USS Laffey's heroic WWII battle against kamikazes at Okinawa.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at some of the rescue ships of the past 200 years of wartime and the brave men and women who risked their lives to save others.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at experimental ships and revolutionary designers that have changed the tide of war through the centuries.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how technological advances over the centuries have transformed maritime warfare.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Vessels that operate in secret, unseen by their enemies, from secret missions that helped win World War II to modern stealth ships using strange shapes and technology to avoid spying ears and eyes.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how maritime task forces and the Coast Guard are keeping commercial vessels and US shores safe from pirates and drug smugglers.

programme.title: D-Day
programme.desc: A retelling of the D-Day invasion and its critical naval operation involving an armada of diverse and ingenious vessels.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: An inside look at the Panavia Tornado's evolution from a Cold War low-level bomber to a high-level fighter-interceptor.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of the Avro Lancaster, the British bomber that was rushed into service to fight back against Nazi Germany.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress is one of the most lethal bombers of the U.S. Air Force in WWII's European and Pacific campaigns; among other things, the plane delivered food to the German-occupied Netherlands.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: A look at the rise and fall of the infamous Nazi dive bomber Ju-87 Stuka, an aircraft of World War II.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The Spitfire's legend and role in the Battle of Britain.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of how the AH/MH-6 evolved over the decades to become an irreplacable aircraft for the U.S. Military.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of Britain's flagship super jet of the Cold War era, the Hawker Hunter, and its most thrilling flights during the late 20th century.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The history of the F-111 and how the fighter-bomber no one wanted went on to dominate both air and battlefields in the late 20th century.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: F6F Hellcat
programme.desc: The naval fighter designed to counter the Japanese Zero turns the tide of the war in the Pacific.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The true story of the F-14 Tomcat, the military aircraft made famous in "Top Gun"; how it served as the US Navy's top carrier fighter for a generation.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The massive AC-130 provides unparalleled close air support to U.S. and allied ground troops.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: An inside look at the P47 Thunderbolt and the critical role it played in both the European and Pacific Theaters of World War II.

programme.desc: The Marine Corps' V-22 Osprey.

programme.desc: The F-15 Eagle's conquests in Iraq, Yugoslavia and even space.

programme.desc: A soaring tour over Southern California explores the stunning beaches, vast deserts, sprawling city and unique culture.

programme.desc: A coast-to-coast tour over America's man-made structures, old and new; the Statue of Liberty and the Golden Gate Bridge.

programme.desc: Taking an aerial tour of Virginia.

programme.desc: A high-flying tour of Connecticut; exploring the safe harbors, buildings and rich maritime history; the home state of Mark Twain, PT Barnum and Katharine Hepburn; the exclusive Gold Coast, the Thimble Islands and the historic cities.

programme.desc: From battlefields where hundreds died to Beale Street where musical legends were born.

programme.desc: Northern state's green forests; red-painted barns and sugar maples.

programme.desc: An aerial view of Arkansas, where settlers' westward journeys began and the scene of Bonnie and Clyde's most serious crime; a view of the land of raging rivers, vast wilderness and legends of the Wild West.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Pilots of Southern Airways Flight 242 must try to land on a Georgia highway after the engines die and the windshield shatters.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Chalk's Ocean Airways flight 101 crashes just after take-off from Watson Island in Miami on December 19, 2005, killing 20 people.
